heading: Automated data processing
label: 654a Automated data processing
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsecs. (d)(4) and (f), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: In order for a State to meet the requirements of this section, the State agency administering the State program under this part shall have in operation a single statewide automated data processing and information retrieval system which has the capability to perform the tasks specified in this section with the frequency and in the manner required by or under this part.
controlling and accounting for use of Federal, State, and local funds in carrying out the program; and
maintaining the data necessary to meet Federal reporting requirements under this part on a timely basis.

children.label: A
children.text: to maintain the requisite data on State performance with respect to paternity establishment and child support enforcement in the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: to calculate the paternity establishment percentage for the State for each fiscal year; and

have in place systems controls to ensure the completeness and reliability of, and ready access to, the data described in paragraph (1)(A), and the accuracy of the calculations described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: permit access to and use of data only to the extent necessary to carry out the State program under this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: specify the data which may be used for particular program purposes, and the personnel permitted access to such data.

children.heading: Systems controls
children.label: 2 Systems controls
children.text: Systems controls (such as passwords or blocking of fields) to ensure strict adherence to the policies described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Monitoring of access
children.label: 3 Monitoring of access
children.text: Routine monitoring of access to and use of the automated system, through methods such as audit trails and feedback mechanisms, to guard against and promptly identify unauthorized access or use.

children.heading: Training and information
children.label: 4 Training and information
children.text: Procedures to ensure that all personnel (including State and local agency staff and contractors) who may have access to or be required to use confidential program data are informed of applicable requirements and penalties (including those in section 6103 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986), and are adequately trained in security procedures.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 5 Penalties
children.text: Administrative penalties (up to and including dismissal from employment) for unauthorized access to, or disclosure or use of, confidential data.

children.label: A
children.text: each case in which services are being provided by the State agency under the State plan approved under this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: each support order established or modified in the State on or after .October 1, 1998

children.heading: Linking of local registries
children.label: 2 Linking of local registries
children.text: The State case registry may be established by linking local case registries of support orders through an automated information network, subject to this section.

children.heading: Use of standardized data elements
children.label: 3 Use of standardized data elements
children.text: Such records shall use standardized data elements for both parents (such as names, social security numbers and other uniform identification numbers, dates of birth, and case identification numbers), and contain such other information (such as on case status) as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of monthly (or other periodic) support owed under the order, and other amounts (including arrearages, interest or late payment penalties, and fees) due or overdue under the order;

children.label: B
children.text: any amount described in subparagraph (A) that has been collected;

children.label: C
children.text: the distribution of such collected amounts;

children.label: D
children.text: the birth date and, beginning not later than , the social security number, of any child for whom the order requires the provision of support; andOctober 1, 1999

children.label: E
children.text: the amount of any lien imposed with respect to the order pursuant to .section 666(a)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: information on administrative actions and administrative and judicial proceedings and orders relating to paternity and support;

children.label: B
children.text: information obtained from comparison with Federal, State, or local sources of information;

children.label: C
children.text: information on support collections and distributions; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other relevant information.

children.heading: Information comparisons and other disclosures of information
children.label: f Information comparisons and other disclosures of information

children.heading: Federal Case Registry of Child Support Orders
children.label: 1 Federal Case Registry of Child Support Orders
children.text: Furnishing to the Federal Case Registry of Child Support Orders established under (and update as necessary, with information including notice of expiration of orders) the minimum amount of information on child support cases recorded in the State case registry that is necessary to operate the registry (as specified by the Secretary in regulations).section 653(h) of this title

children.heading: Federal Parent Locator Service
children.label: 2 Federal Parent Locator Service
children.text: Exchanging information with the Federal Parent Locator Service for the purposes specified in .section 653 of this title

children.heading: Temporary family assistance and medicaid agencies
children.label: 3 Temporary family assistance and medicaid agencies
children.text: Exchanging information with State agencies (of the State and of other States) administering programs funded under part A, programs operated under a State plan approved under subchapter XIX, and other programs designated by the Secretary, as necessary to perform State agency responsibilities under this part and under such programs.

children.heading: Intrastate and interstate information comparisons
children.label: 4 Intrastate and interstate information comparisons
children.text: Exchanging information with other agencies of the State, agencies of other States, and interstate information networks, as necessary and appropriate to carry out (or assist other States to carry out) the purposes of this part.

children.heading: Private industry councils receiving welfare-to-work grants
children.label: 5 Private industry councils receiving welfare-to-work grants
children.text: Disclosing to a private industry council (as defined in ) to which funds are provided under the names, addresses, telephone numbers, and identifying case number information in the State program funded under part A, of noncustodial parents residing in the service delivery area of the private industry council, for the purpose of identifying and contacting noncustodial parents regarding participation in the program under .section 603(a)(5)(D)(ii) of this titlesection 603(a)(5) of this titlesection 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Collection and distribution of support payments
children.label: g Collection and distribution of support payments
i
children.text: within 2 business days after receipt of notice of, and the income source subject to, such withholding from a court, another State, an employer, the Federal Parent Locator Service, or another source recognized by the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: using uniform formats prescribed by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: at the option of the employer, using the electronic transmission methods prescribed by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: ongoing monitoring to promptly identify failures to make timely payment of support; and

children.label: C
children.text: automatic use of enforcement procedures (including procedures authorized pursuant to ) if payments are not timely made.section 666(c) of this title

children.heading: “Business day” defined
children.label: 2 “Business day” defined
children.text: As used in paragraph (1), the term “business day” means a day on which State offices are open for regular business.

children.heading: Expedited administrative procedures
children.label: h Expedited administrative procedures
children.text: The automated system required by this section shall be used, to the maximum extent feasible, to implement the expedited administrative procedures required by .section 666(c) of this title
